name: Sashimi (Sliced Raw Fish)
recipeInstructions:
Dipping Sauce:
4 Tbsp Soy Sauce -OR-
1 tbsp Tosa Joyu
1 tbsp Chirizu
Garnish:
1-2 inch section of Daikon radish (carrot or celery optional)
Cutting the Fish:
There are 4 basic cuts for Sashimi. A very sharp, heavy knife is required.
1. Hara Giri (Flat Cut) - Most popular cut. Suitable for any filleted fish. Hold the fish firmly and slice straight down into slices about 1/4 to 1/2 inch thick and 1 inch wide, depending on the size of the fillet.
2. Kaku Giri (Cubic Cut) - This style is often used for tuna fish. Slice as above, then cut the slices into 1/2-inch cubes.
3. Ito Zukuri (Thread Shape) - Although this technique can be used with any small fish, it's especially suitable for squid. Slice the squid straight down into 1/4-inch slices, and then slice lengthwise into 1/4-inch wide strips.
4. Usu Zukuri (Paper Thin Slices) - Place a fillet of bass or bream on a flat surface and hold the fish firmly with one hand. Slice it at an angle to create almost transparent sheets.
To Serve:
Sashimi can be composed of one type of fish or multiple types. Arrange the fish attractively on individual serving plates. Garnish each plate with about 1/2 teaspoon of wasabi and decorate with strips of daikon, carrot, and/or celery. Cover with a sheet of plastic wrap and refrigerate for no more than 1 hour before serving. Pour the chosen dipping sauce into small dishes and accompany each serving of Sashimi with its own sauce. Mix the wasabi with soy sauce or Tosa Joyu to taste. To serve as an appetizer, arrange two or more varieties or cuts of sashimi on a serving dish and accompany with dipping sauce.
